title: ranunculus aquatilis var. baudotii
intro: Ranunculus aquatilis var. baudotii, also known as Baudot's water crowfoot, is a floating aquatic plant belonging to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae) and is native to northern Europe and Asia. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to reduce inflammation and act as an antiseptic, making it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Ranunculus aquatilis var. baudotii include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to prevent cell damage and reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as cancer and heart disease. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to take advantage of its medicinal properties, although it should be used with caution due to its potential toxicity in large doses.
